verses.text: Pray that the word may 'have free course,' and that we be delivered from unreasonable men. Free course. He asks prayer for the gospel's spread.
Pray that the word may 'have free course,' and that we be delivered from unreasonable men. Free course. He asks prayer for the gospel's spread.
'The Lord is faithful, who shall stablish you, and keep you from evil'; the Lord direct your hearts. Faithful Lord. He guards His own from the evil one.
'The Lord is faithful, who shall stablish you, and keep you from evil'; the Lord direct your hearts. Faithful Lord. He guards His own from the evil one.
'The Lord is faithful, who shall stablish you, and keep you from evil'; the Lord direct your hearts. Faithful Lord. He guards His own from the evil one.
Withdraw from the disorderly; we wrought with labour, 'not... chargeable to any.' An example. Paul worked to model honest diligence.
Withdraw from the disorderly; we wrought with labour, 'not... chargeable to any.' An example. Paul worked to model honest diligence.
Withdraw from the disorderly; we wrought with labour, 'not... chargeable to any.' An example. Paul worked to model honest diligence.
Withdraw from the disorderly; we wrought with labour, 'not... chargeable to any.' An example. Paul worked to model honest diligence.
'If any would not work, neither should he eat'; with quietness work, and eat their own bread. Work rule. Idleness has no place in the church.
'If any would not work, neither should he eat'; with quietness work, and eat their own bread. Work rule. Idleness has no place in the church.
'If any would not work, neither should he eat'; with quietness work, and eat their own bread. Work rule. Idleness has no place in the church.
'Be not weary in well doing'; if any obey not, note him, yet 'count him not as an enemy, but admonish him.' Firm yet kind. Discipline aims at recovery.
'Be not weary in well doing'; if any obey not, note him, yet 'count him not as an enemy, but admonish him.' Firm yet kind. Discipline aims at recovery.
'Be not weary in well doing'; if any obey not, note him, yet 'count him not as an enemy, but admonish him.' Firm yet kind. Discipline aims at recovery.
'The Lord of peace... give you peace always'; the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Benediction. He closes with peace and grace.
